result:
**Goal: Add a Solar System Orrery simulation mode.**

The project is a 42K-line terminal-based simulation engine (`life.py`) with 65+ modes spanning cellular automata, physics, fluid dynamics, and more. Recent commits have been adding earth/space science simulations (tectonic plates, weather, ocean currents, volcanoes, black hole accretion disk).

The natural next addition: a **Solar System Orrery** — an overhead view of the solar system with all 8 planets orbiting the Sun using Keplerian orbital mechanics. It would feature accurate relative orbital periods, elliptical orbits with eccentricity, an inner/outer solar system zoom toggle, comet/asteroid belt visualization, and planet info overlays. This complements the existing N-body gravity mode (which is abstract) and the black hole mode (which is relativistic) by providing a concrete, recognizable astronomical simulation.
